scales.title: Styles
scales.subtitle: s.typography.grouped
scales.purpose: Composite text styles used mainly in component build process. Static does not react to variable modes; dynamic scales with the ui_scale mode (sm / md / lg).
scales.guideline: Pair Static with hard-coded layouts; pair Dynamic with anything that should scale across density tiers.

html.title: Styles
html.subtitle: h.typography.style  ·  h.link
html.purpose: Text styles for common page content: headings, body / paragraph styles, and link interaction states. All consume the typography_context variable — set the frame's mode to switch interface ↔ prose.
html.guideline: Pair semantically with HTML elements (h1–h6, p, a). To compare interface vs prose, set the typography_context mode on each table frame manually in Figma.

section.title: Grouped Composites — Static
section.purpose: T-shirt sized composite text styles (font-family + weight + size + line-height + letter-spacing + paragraph-spacing) that do NOT change with the ui_scale mode.
section.guideline: Use for any text whose typographic dimensions must remain constant across ui_scale (sm / md / lg). Pair with the dimension foundation when laying out static layouts.

section.title: Grouped Composites — Dynamic
section.purpose: T-shirt sized composite text styles that scale with the active ui_scale mode. Previewed at the page default (md). Switch the ui_scale mode on the containing frame to inspect sm or lg.
section.guideline: Use as the default body and label styles. Renders at the active ui_scale mode wherever applied.

section.title: HTML — Headings
section.purpose: Heading text styles (H1–H6) for HTML document contexts. Switches between `interface` and `prose` typography contexts.
section.guideline: Pair semantically with HTML <h1>…<h6> elements. Heading scale comes from typography_context — set the parent's context mode to switch interface ↔ prose.

section.title: HTML — Body
section.purpose: Body / paragraph / link text styles used in HTML document contexts (interface and prose).
section.guideline: Default body style is `normal`; `strong` matches <strong>/<b>; `lead` is for opening paragraphs; `link` covers inline links.

section.title: HTML — Link States
section.purpose: Text styles for the six interaction states of an inline text link.
section.guideline: Match the interaction state to its real-world trigger. Pressed wins over hover; disabled wins over everything else.

section.title: Component-specific Styles
section.purpose: Text styles owned by individual components (badge, infobox, popover, tooltip). Each binds to a higher-level role on that component.
section.guideline: Component-local styles never leak outside their owning component. Add new entries here when introducing a new component-only role.
